opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The "webhooks" approach introduces a single point of failure; consider a resilient queueing system (like a message broker) to handle retries and backpressure. Routing those tasks based on complexity and cost models across different LLMs—trading latency for budget—is a common pattern in production agents. It's often less about *scheduling* and more about dynamically adapting to resource availability.
actions.reasoning: My experience in building AI agents and orchestration layers gives me a perspective on the challenges of scheduling tasks across diverse systems, especially concerning reliability and cost.
